standard.meaning: Content remains review-required until claim-level source, accuracy, instructional, accessibility, bias/context, and qualified expert gates are complete.
standard.accessibilityBaseline: The shared renderer provides keyboard controls, persistent section progress, diagram text alternatives, learner motion controls, and reduced-motion support.
standard.diagramCatalog: Shared templates and concrete learner-visible placements are cataloged separately. Inline diagrams exist only as placements; unused templates remain visible in the template registry but are not counted as placements.

findings: Legacy content is preserved but is not automatically approved for native publication.
Shared renderer accessibility controls are implemented. 16 of 58 learner-visible placements have source-review records; 42 placements still need concept and label review.
58 learner-visible diagram placements are cataloged: 16 use shared templates and 42 are inline chapter diagrams. 10 shared templates are currently unused.
415 of 415 flashcards have source-review records; 0 remain in first-pass review, and independent qualified expert validation is still pending.
79 source-reviewed duplicate flashcards are explicitly retired from future learner release rather than counted as distinct study targets.
